qa_pairs.question: After creating an image of a volume on Legacy Arbutus, how do you transfer that image to the New Arbutus Cloud and use it to create a new volume?
qa_pairs.answer: First, in the Legacy Arbutus session, download the image using the command `openstack image save --file <filename> <image-id>`. Next, in the New Arbutus Cloud session on the migration host, upload the image using `openstack --os-cloud <cloud> image create --private --file <file> <newImageName>`. Finally, to create a volume from this uploaded image, navigate to Compute -> Images in the New Arbutus Cloud web UI, select the uploaded image, and choose 'Create Volume' from the drop-down menu. This new volume can then be attached to existing instances or used to boot a new instance.

qa_pairs.question: What is the process for creating an image from a volume attached to a temporary migration host on Legacy Arbutus using the `dd` utility, as an alternative method for migrating volume-backed instances?
qa_pairs.answer: After launching a temporary migration host instance on Legacy Arbutus, deleting the original instance (but retaining its volume), and attaching that volume to the temporary host, use the `dd` utility. For example, if the volume is attached as `/dev/vdb`, the command `dd if=/dev/vdb | openstack image create --private --container-format bare --disk-format raw "volumemigrate"` will create an image. This newly created image will then be visible under Compute -> Images in the New Arbutus Cloud web UI and can be used to launch instances, ensuring a new volume is created if data persistence is desired.

qa_pairs.question: When migrating large volumes between Legacy Arbutus and New Arbutus where image-based methods are not recommended, how can the Linux `dd` command be used to create an identical copy of a bootable volume?
qa_pairs.answer: To migrate large bootable volumes, first create temporary instances on both Legacy and New Arbutus, and assign them floating IP addresses for SSH access. Attach the volume you want to copy from Legacy Arbutus to its temporary instance and an empty volume of the same size to the temporary New Arbutus instance. Then, on the temporary Legacy Arbutus instance, execute a command within a `screen` session like `sudo dd bs=16M if=/dev/vdb | pv -s 96G | ssh -i key.pem user@xxx.xx.xx.xx "sudo dd bs=16M of=/dev/vdb"`. This command streams an exact copy of the volume from Legacy Arbutus directly to the new volume on Arbutus Cloud, which can then be used to launch instances.
